join_order.description: nation (25 rows) → direct array[25] by n_nationkey. No join needed.
join_order.probe: n/a — direct array lookup by s_nationkey during lineitem scan

join_order.description: supplier (100K rows) → direct array[100001] of s_nationkey by s_suppkey. Suppkey range 1–100K.
join_order.probe: n/a — direct array lookup by l_suppkey during lineitem scan

join_order.description: part (2M rows) filtered by p_name LIKE '%green%' → 100K qualifying partkeys → bitset(2M bits=256KB).
join_order.probe: n/a — bitset check on l_partkey during lineitem scan

join_order.description: mmap pre-built ps_key_hash index for (l_partkey, l_suppkey) → ps_supplycost lookup.
join_order.probe: qualifying lineitem rows (~3M) probe via composite key (l_partkey<<32|l_suppkey)

join_order.description: mmap pre-built o_orderkey_hash index for l_orderkey → o_orderdate lookup.
join_order.probe: qualifying lineitem rows (~3M) probe by l_orderkey

join_order.description: lineitem (60M rows) is the single probe scan; all dimension lookups happen inline per qualifying row.
join_order.build: n/a — lineitem is always the probe side
join_order.probe: lineitem (60M rows scanned, ~3M pass bitset filter)

filter_pushdown.action: build bitset(2000001) of qualifying p_partkey values
skip 95% of lineitem rows early in inner loop before any hash index probe

data_structures.part_filter: bitset(2000001) — 256KB, fits in L3; exact filter, no false positives; built by scanning part 2M rows with p_name substring check
data_structures.supplier_lookup: direct_array[100001] of int32_t (s_nationkey) — indexed by s_suppkey (range 1–100K); 400KB, L3-resident; O(1) zero-overhead lookup by l_suppkey
data_structures.nation_lookup: direct_array[25] of string index (int8_t) — indexed by n_nationkey (range 0–24); decode via n_name_dict.txt at output time
data_structures.partsupp_lookup: mmap ps_key_hash (pre-built, 16M slots × 16B = 256MB); composite key int64_t k=(l_partkey<<32|l_suppkey); hash=fibonacci; probed only for ~3M qualifying lineitems
data_structures.orders_lookup: mmap o_orderkey_hash (pre-built, 33.5M slots × 8B = 268MB); key=l_orderkey; hash=fibonacci; probed only for ~3M qualifying lineitems
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local double profit[25][7] — flat 2D array indexed by [nation_code][year-1992]; 175 entries × 8B = 1.4KB per thread; zero contention; 64 threads × 1.4KB = ~90KB total; trivial sequential merge

parallelism.phase_1_build: sequential — nation array load (25 rows), supplier array load (100K rows), part bitset build (2M rows scan); all fast, no parallelism needed
parallelism.phase_2_index_load: mmap ps_key_hash + o_orderkey_hash — O(1) OS page faults on demand, no explicit build
parallelism.phase_3_lineitem_scan: OpenMP parallel for over lineitem morsels (100K rows each); each thread: bitset check → ps_key_hash probe → supplier array lookup → nation array lookup → o_orderkey_hash probe → year extract → thread-local profit[n][y] accumulate
parallelism.phase_4_merge: sequential merge of 64 thread-local profit[25][7] arrays — 64×175 additions, negligible
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge — no locks, no atomics during scan phase; merge is sequential post-scan

index_usage.purpose: O(1) lookup of ps_supplycost given (l_partkey, l_suppkey) — avoids full 8M-row partsupp scan
index_usage.slot_format: HashSlot64 {int64_t key, uint32_t row_pos, uint32_t _pad} = 16B; empty: key==INT64_MIN

index_usage.purpose: O(1) lookup of o_orderdate given l_orderkey — avoids full 15M-row orders scan
index_usage.slot_format: HashSlot {int32_t key, uint32_t row_pos} = 8B; empty: key==INT32_MIN

index_usage.purpose: NOT USED — supplier (100K rows, suppkey 1–100K) loaded as direct array instead; array lookup is 10-50x faster than hash probe
NOT USED — we build a runtime bitset of qualifying partkeys from the part scan; bitset check is cheaper than hash lookup for a 100K-key filter

scan_column_access.partsupp: accessed only via ps_key_hash mmap index → ps_supplycost column
scan_column_access.orders: accessed only via o_orderkey_hash mmap index → o_orderdate column

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for all column scans; random_access_with_prefetch for ps_key_hash and o_orderkey_hash probes (only ~3M probes each after bitset filter, manageable despite index size exceeding L3)
physical_plan.year_extraction: linear scan over 7 years (1992–1998) per o_orderdate value; use YEAR_DAYS[] lookup array from query guide
physical_plan.late_materialization: p_name string data read during part scan (required for filter); no other string materialization until final output stage where nation name decoded from n_name_dict.txt

phase_breakdown.part_scan_bitset_build: ~10ms (2M rows, sequential, fast string strstr scan)
phase_breakdown.lineitem_parallel_scan: ~120ms (60M rows / 64 threads = ~937K rows/thread; bitset cuts probes to 3M; two random-access hash index probes per qualifying row)

estimated_cost.target_comparison: Umbra=196ms, DuckDB=253ms; plan targets sub-200ms via 64-thread morsel scan + mmap index reuse + direct array aggregation (zero contention on 175-group flat array)
